metadata.title: Profile web research 2026-07-15 batch 6A
metadata.notes: Public-web research only. Primary institutional, patent, and plant-variety-rights records were preferred. Historical patents and PBR records remain attributed to the rightsholder shown for that filing; later employment or company formation is not treated as a transfer of title. No external communications were made.

profiles.ownershipType: private family-owned breeding and fruit-production company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Rusticas del Guadalquivir S.L. is the family-owned breeding entity that founded and operates within the Royal/SAT Royal fruit group. Official Spanish variety records continue to name Rusticas del Guadalquivir S.L. as breeder and rightsholder for recent LOR and VER peach selections. Royal is the operating group and commercial brand context; the available records do not support replacing Rusticas with Royal as the owner of every historical filing.
profiles.headquarters: Hacienda la Jarilla, 41300 San Jose de la Rinconada, Sevilla, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: Royal identifies founder Jose Gandia as president, Macarena Garcia-Otero as CEO, and Pascal Boutin as general manager. This is a multigenerational family enterprise with professional operating leadership and an active internal breeding program.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Group contact for Rusticas/Royal breeding activities
profiles.trademarkStatus: official brand-use evidence; registry ownership not independently checked

webResearchSources.label: Spanish Ministry of Agriculture variety bulletin naming Rusticas as owner and breeder
Royal research and breeding program
Royal leadership team

profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat the exact record as the company Rusticas del Guadalquivir S.L., not as an individual or a malformed name. Royal group branding is evidence of operating affiliation, not a blanket patent assignment.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Jose Martin Charlez Martinez is a Spanish fruit-variety breeder and the current technical-commercial director listed by VIF Internacional. A February 2026 corporate filing also names him sole administrator of the newly formed Varietal Innovation Fruit S.L. Historical Spanish registry entries for NETIX, MELOX, and REDIX selections identify PROSEPLAN S.L. as breeder or representative, so those older records must not be reassigned to VIF or the 2026 company without transaction evidence.
profiles.headquarters: Avenida Fuerzas Armadas s/n, 50300 Calatayud, Zaragoza, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: The current VIF role and sole-administrator filing indicate founder-led or key-person leadership. The public record supports continuity in breeding activity, but it does not establish that the newer VIF entity acquired every right formerly associated with Proseplan.
profiles.trademarkStatus: cultivar and commercial-name use documented; trademark ownership not independently verified

webResearchSources.label: Spanish variety registry entries for Proseplan NETIX, MELOX, and REDIX selections
VIF Internacional public contact and current role
February 2026 BORME filing for Varietal Innovation Fruit S.L.

profiles.webResearchNotes: Identity is resolved, but the ownership timeline must remain split: Proseplan appears in historical registry records; VIF is the current public affiliation; Varietal Innovation Fruit S.L. is a later 2026 company.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Takeshi Kihara is a former Japanese public-sector fruit breeder associated with the National Institute of Fruit Tree Science and later NARO publications. The Akizuki pear patent names him as an inventor but records the National Institute of Fruit Tree Science, Ministry of Agriculture, as assignee. NARO cultivar literature likewise treats the work as an institutional breeding output, not Kihara's personal property.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Kihara is identified as a former institutional contributor. Ongoing licensing and commercialization are handled through NARO's institutional intellectual-property function rather than through a personal breeder business.

webResearchSources.label: Akizuki pear patent inventor and assignee record
NARO Hatsumaru cultivar paper identifying Kihara as a former contributor
NARO licensing program

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact name is an individual, not a company. Public-institution assignment controls the cited patent; no personal rightsholder claim was found.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Terrance C. Moran is documented as a strawberry breeder for Driscoll's. The Canadian PBR record for DrisStrawTwentySeven names Moran and Michael Ferguson as breeders while naming Driscoll's, Inc. as applicant and holder. The record supports corporate ownership rather than personal ownership by Moran.
profiles.headquarters: Watsonville, California, United States (Driscoll's corporate contact)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Moran is supported as a historical Driscoll's breeder, but a current role or personal breeding business was not established. Portfolio continuity sits with Driscoll's and its breeding organization.
profiles.trademarkStatus: trade name documented in PBR record

webResearchSources.label: Canadian PBR record for DrisStrawTwentySeven / Driscoll's Marquis
Driscoll's official contact path

profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not interpret breeder credit as personal title. Current employment was not uniquely established, so the profile is limited to the supported historical Driscoll's relationship.

profiles.ownershipType: individual former public-research founder and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Jaroslav Tupy was the founding scientific leader of the apple-breeding program at the Institute of Experimental Botany of the Czech Academy of Sciences. Patents such as UEB 3264/2 name Tupy as an inventor and the Institute of Experimental Botany as assignee. The institute, not Tupy personally, is the supported rightsholder for the cited institutional portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: Strizovice, Czechia (breeding station); Prague, Czechia (institute headquarters)
profiles.leadershipSummary: The institute describes Tupy as its late founder. Radek Cerny joined the program in 2009, learned from Tupy, and became head of the breeding station in 2016, providing a documented institutional succession path.

webResearchSources.label: UEB founder profile for Jaroslav Tupy
UEB team and succession information
UEB 3264/2 patent inventor and assignee record

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact record is an individual founder within a public-research institution. Institutional cultivar and patent ownership should not be converted into personal ownership.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact string is not uniquely resolvable. UEB history identifies Otto Louda Sr., born in 1913, as a founder and private breeder of older cultivars including Sampion, Rubin, Jonalord, and Melodie; the same history says those unprotected classic varieties were not UEB property. Later UEB materials and patents also identify an Ing. Otto Louda in the institutional breeding team. A filing date or patent number is required to distinguish the generations and determine the correct ownership relationship.
profiles.headquarters: Strizovice and Prague, Czechia (institutional context only; identity unresolved)
profiles.leadershipSummary: No single succession or current-status statement is safe for this canonical string. Older private breeding and later institute-assigned work must remain separate until the underlying record is dated and normalized.

webResearchSources.label: UEB history distinguishing founder Otto Louda Sr. and older private cultivars
Official apple-breeding station history
Later UEB patent record naming Otto Louda and the institute as assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not merge Otto Louda Sr.'s older private breeding with the later institutional inventor record. This profile remains unresolved by design because the exact canonical name omits a generation marker.

profiles.ownershipType: malformed inverted-name record for an individual historical corporate breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The record is a malformed inverted rendering of Harold A. Johnson, Jr. Plant patents identify Johnson as a historical Driscoll Strawberry Associates breeder and record assignment of the inventors' interests to Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Inc. Breeder credit does not establish personal ownership.
profiles.headquarters: Watsonville, California, United States (historical Driscoll relationship)
profiles.leadershipSummary: The supported activity is a legacy corporate breeding role from the 1970s through the early 1990s. No current personal enterprise or successor was established; the supported portfolio continuity is through Driscoll's.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Company contact for the historical Driscoll portfolio

webResearchSources.label: Commander patent naming Harold A. Johnson, Jr. and recording assignment to Driscoll
Driscoll's official contact path

profiles.webResearchNotes: Normalize for matching as Harold A. Johnson, Jr. while preserving the supplied canonicalName. Do not treat the malformed string as a separate breeder or the old patents as personally held.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Jan Zima is a long-serving member of the apple-breeding team at the Institute of Experimental Botany of the Czech Academy of Sciences. UEB patents name him as an inventor while assigning title to the Institute of Experimental Botany. His breeder credit is therefore institutional, not evidence of personal ownership.
profiles.headquarters: Strizovice, Czechia (breeding station); Prague, Czechia (institute headquarters)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Zima remains listed as a senior team member with decades of practical breeding and orchard experience. The program is led by Radek Cerny, head since 2016, providing institutional continuity beyond any one breeder.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Breeding-team member, UEB apple breeding station

webResearchSources.label: UEB team profile and public contact for Jan Zima
UEB 3264/2 patent inventor and assignee record
UEB program history and portfolio

profiles.webResearchNotes: Use the institute as the rightsholder for institute-assigned filings. The direct email and phone are public institutional business contacts from the official team page.

profiles.ownershipType: individual public-university professor emeritus and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Mikeal L. Roose is a University of California, Riverside citrus geneticist and professor emeritus. UCR patent and licensing records place title and propagation rights for cited cultivars with The Regents of the University of California, not with Roose personally. His current emeritus status does not transfer those historical rights.
profiles.headquarters: University of California, Riverside, Riverside, California, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Roose is emeritus but remains publicly connected to citrus research. UCR's citrus program and licensing function continue through a broader team that includes Tim Williams and current citrus researchers, reducing dependence on a single retired breeder.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Professor emeritus and geneticist, UC Riverside
profiles.trademarkStatus: official market-brand examples documented; trademark owners may be third parties

webResearchSources.label: UC Riverside faculty profile for Mikeal Roose
UC Riverside Tango licensing and rights page
UC Riverside 2026 Tango legacy and market-brand article

profiles.webResearchNotes: The cultivar portfolio is university-held or university-managed. Cuties, Halos, and Tango Fruit are documented market brands in the UCR article but are not attributed to Roose as trademark owner.

profiles.ownershipType: individual retired government-research director and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Donato Antonacci is a retired Italian government grape researcher and former director of CREA's Turi unit. CREA's innovation records name Antonacci as breeder while identifying CREA as rights holder for cultivars such as Apenestae and Egnatia N. Retirement and later unpaid scientific activity do not move those rights to Antonacci personally.
profiles.headquarters: Turi, Bari, Italy (historical CREA viticulture unit); CREA headquarters in Rome, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: Antonacci retired from his director role effective March 2017 and continued in scientific activity. CREA retains institutional ownership and technology-transfer capability; current cultivar pages identify Rocco Perniola and CREA technology transfer as the commercialization path.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Institutional contact for CREA plant-variety rights

webResearchSources.label: CREA retirement decree for Donato Antonacci
CREA innovation record for Egnatia N naming holder and breeder
CREA table-grape event showing Antonacci's later scientific role

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact record is an individual, but the supported rightsholder is CREA. Public contact information is institutional technology-transfer information, not private contact data.

profiles.ownershipType: individual public-university professor emeritus and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: James J. Luby, also publicly styled Jim Luby, is professor emeritus and the former director of the University of Minnesota fruit-breeding program. University licensing and PBR records identify the Regents of the University of Minnesota, sometimes jointly with USDA for specific germplasm, as holder or licensing principal. Luby's breeder credit does not constitute personal title.
profiles.headquarters: University of Minnesota, St. Paul, Minnesota, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Luby retired in 2024 after leading the fruit-breeding program from 1982 through 2024. Matthew Clark took over leadership of the fruit-breeding program, providing a clear institutional succession signal.

webResearchSources.label: University of Minnesota profile for Jim Luby
University of Minnesota announcement of Matthew Clark's succession
University licensing page for MN55 / First Kiss / Rave

profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep university and any USDA co-held rights at the institutional level. Retirement is a leadership transition, not an ownership transfer.

profiles.ownershipType: individual former public-university breeder and later independent grower
profiles.ownershipSummary: Gray E. Martin was a UC Riverside staff research assistant in the avocado-breeding program from April 1984 through March 1997 and later continued as an independent grower and breeder. The Lamb/Hass patent names Martin as an inventor and The Regents of the University of California as assignee. Later independent horticultural work must not be used to reassign the historical UCR avocado portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: Southern California, United States; no formal current company headquarters verified
profiles.leadershipSummary: The public record supports a completed UCR staff career followed by individual grower/breeder activity. No formal current company, management bench, or documented business successor was uniquely verified; UCR retains institutional continuity for the historical avocado program.
profiles.trademarkStatus: cultivar and licensed commercial-name evidence; trademark ownership not independently verified

webResearchSources.label: UC Riverside contributor history for Gray Martin
Lamb/Hass patent inventor and Regents assignee record
UC Riverside announcement of GEM avocado licensing

profiles.webResearchNotes: This profile deliberately separates historical UCR work from later independent breeding. No private address or personal contact information was collected.

profiles.ownershipType: individual former public-university breeder and current private consultant
profiles.ownershipSummary: Stefano Lugli is an Italian cherry breeder formerly associated with the University of Bologna and currently active through SL Fruit Service. The University of Bologna states that its listed plant-variety rights are university-owned and names Lugli, Roberto Grandi, and Mirella Correale as breeders of the Sweet Series. Lugli's later consultancy does not replace the university as owner of those historical rights.
profiles.headquarters: Modena, Italy (SL Fruit Service); Bologna, Italy (historical university rights holder)
profiles.leadershipSummary: SL Fruit Service, founded in 2023, appears founder-led around Lugli's expertise. The University of Bologna retains an institutional knowledge-transfer office and continuing plant-variety portfolio, providing continuity for the earlier university-owned cultivars.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Founder, SL Fruit Service; scientific committee coordinator, Cherry Times
profiles.trademarkStatus: commercial marks documented in university portfolio materials

webResearchSources.label: University of Bologna plant-variety-rights portfolio and breeder credits
Cherry Times scientific committee profile
Public SL Fruit Service business contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact individual is distinguished from similarly named academics by the cherry-breeding portfolio and current SL Fruit Service role. Historical Sweet Series rights remain with the University of Bologna.

profiles.ownershipType: individual government-research program leader and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Nir Carmi is an active citrus breeder and program manager at Israel's Agricultural Research Organization, Volcani Center. Official program and patent/PBR records identify the ARO or the State of Israel, Ministry of Agriculture, as applicant or institutional rights principal for cited selections. Carmi is the breeder and program lead, not the supported personal rightsholder.
profiles.headquarters: Hamakabim Road 68, P.O. Box 15159, Rishon LeZion 7528809, Israel
profiles.leadershipSummary: Carmi currently leads the citrus-breeding program within a multi-researcher public institution. The official project page shows a broad cultivar pipeline and an institutional team, supporting continuity beyond one breeder.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Citrus breeding program manager, ARO Volcani Center

webResearchSources.label: ARO Volcani researcher profile and public business contact
ARO Volcani citrus-breeding program and cultivar portfolio
Moroccan PBR bulletin naming Ministry/ARO applicant and Carmi among breeders

profiles.webResearchNotes: All contact details are official institutional business contacts. Keep ARO/State ownership separate from Carmi's inventor and breeder attribution.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Nicola Tufaro is a shareholder and strawberry breeder at Nova Siri Genetics S.r.l. Patent and PBR records for cited cultivars identify Nova Siri Genetics as applicant, assignee, or holder while naming Tufaro as breeder or inventor. The supported commercial rightsholder is the company, not Tufaro personally.
profiles.headquarters: Via San Maurizio 46, 75025 Policoro, Matera, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: Nova Siri Genetics identifies Pasquale Casalnuovo as sole administrator, Carmela Suriano as director, Jose Miguel Arias as co-founder, and Tufaro as shareholder and breeder. This is an active company with operating and technical leadership beyond the breeder alone.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Company contact for breeding and licensing
profiles.trademarkStatus: official registered-symbol brand use; separate registry ownership not independently checked

webResearchSources.label: Nova Siri Genetics research program and breeder role
Melissa patent naming Tufaro and Nova Siri Genetics
Nova Siri Genetics official contact page

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact name is an individual within Nova Siri Genetics. Company title is supported for the cited portfolio; no personal ownership claim is made.
